reviews.review_msg: Isabel is my favourite SFU professor, I always looked forward to attending her lectures. I was a new student at SFU, but she was very helpful with placement and supporting my learning. She genuinely cares about her students and is very kind. The movies we watched were interesting, and this class helped me improve my Spanish a lot. Highly recommend!

Although a fair marker, she continuously mentioned the false idea that Spain helped the Indigenous in the Americas and that they benefitted from Spain. She fully tries to diminish the severity of the horrific and tragic impacts the people in the Americas went through. It's actually insane how ignorant and demeaning she is about it.

Literally have no bad things to say about Isabel. She's caring, insightful, and puts a lot of effort into making classes interesting and engaging. I'd recommend her to anyone looking to work on their Spanish.

Fantastic professor. First time I've ever had a prof encourage you to reach out for extra help and actually mean it. Classes are fun, prof is caring and engaging, and the textbook is pretty straightforward.
I loved this class. Most of the movies were great, with some exceptions. You have to participate in online discussions, which takes time, but I really like to read others opinions.
